response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack generalist with a genuine testing culture and some high-throughput systems exposure, but he badly misses the core minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++; his verifiable C++ experience is roughly one year (a 7-month stint at National Instruments plus a thesis project), and C++ does not even appear in his skills list. His large-scale infrastructure experience (~2 years of low-latency DLang microservices at Dunnhumby) falls well short of the 5-year bar, and his leadership evidence is informal mentoring and workshops rather than owning technical strategy, priorities, and cross-team delivery for a platform.
strengths: Strong testing and launch track record: TDD practitioner who authored test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and has shipped products across web, mobile, and embedded for 15+ years
Real high-throughput systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic: low-latency async I/O services handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing: multi-day Selenium courses, workshops, conference talks, and code katas
concerns: Roughly 1 year of professional C++ against a hard 8-year minimum; C++ is absent from his current skills list
Large-scale infrastructure and distributed-systems experience (~2 years) is well below the 5-year requirement, with a career centered on web/full-stack work
No formal technical-leadership role owning platform strategy, project priorities, deadlines, or cross-team alignment
